job_description:
We are hiring a highly skilled C# .NET Desktop Developer to build, optimize, and maintain high-performance Windows desktop applications. You will take ownership of core application modules, improve responsiveness and stability, and work closely with stakeholders to deliver reliable features end-to-end—from UI to database. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving complex problems, tuning performance, and writing clean, maintainable code

 

Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain Windows desktop applications using C# and .NET (WinForms).

Build and enhance features end-to-end (UI, business logic, database integration).

Optimize performance (startup time, UI responsiveness, memory usage, CPU utilization).

Implement robust data access and business logic with clean architecture and separation of concerns.

Design and maintain database schemas, tables, indexes, and queries for performance and scalability.

Write clean, testable, maintainable code and produce clear technical documentation.

Troubleshoot production issues, identify root causes, and deliver timely fixes.

Collaborate with product/operations teams to refine requirements and deliver solutions.

Participate in code reviews and contribute to engineering standards and best practices.

Use Git-based workflows and support release cycles.
job_requirements:
Required Qualifications
Strong professional experience in C# and .NET building desktop applications.
Solid hands-on experience with WinForms (controls, layouts, event-driven programming, custom UI components).
Strong knowledge of SQL and database design (normalization, indexing, query optimization).
Excellent debugging skills and ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
Strong understanding of OOP principles and maintainable application architecture.
Experience with Git and collaborative development practices.
Experience building maintainable architectures (layered architecture, clean separation of concerns).

 

Nice to Have (Preferred Qualifications)
Experience with WPF or modernizing legacy WinForms apps.
Experience with .NET 6/7/8 and migrating from older .NET Framework versions.
Experience with ORMs such as Entity Framework or Dapper (and understanding performance tradeoffs).
Knowledge of async programming, multi-threading, and UI thread safety.
Familiarity with CI/CD ( GitHub Actions, etc.).
Secure coding practices and handling sensitive data.
Experience with reporting tools, charts, and data-heavy desktop UI.

 

Technical Skills (What You’ll Use)
C# / .NET, WinForms, OOP, design patterns
SQL & database schema design, performance tuning, indexing (DB2/ PostgreSQL / MySQL)
Debugging and profiling tools (Visual Studio diagnostics, profilers)
Version control (Git), issue tracking, documentation

 

Soft Skills
Strong ownership mindset and accountability for deliverables
Excellent communication and ability to explain technical topics clearly
Detail-oriented with a focus on quality and maintainability
Ability to prioritize, estimate tasks, and meet deadlines

Your Impact:

As a Software Developer at Renewable Energy Pros, you will play a pivotal role in shaping and advancing our cutting-edge energy storage software platform. This position combines design sensibility with technical expertise, empowering you to lead the development of intuitive user interfaces, conceptualize and implement innovative features, and ensure our platform evolves in response to client needs. Apply knowledge of UI design and software engineering to design and develop new user interfaces and interaction models using the Python Pyside framework.

At Storlytics, you’ll be part of a dynamic and innovative team driving meaningful change in renewable energy.

job_description:
Job Purpose:

We are seeking a proactive and customer-focused IT Help Desk to serve as the first point of contact for our employees' technical issues. You will be responsible for providing timely and effective technical assistance, resolving problems with hardware, software, and network systems, and ensuring a seamless technology experience for our end-users. The ideal candidate is a problem-solver with excellent communication skills and a passion for technology.

 

Key Responsibilities:

Technical Support & Troubleshooting (70%)

  Provide first-line technical support via phone, email, chat, and in-person requests.
  Diagnose and resolve hardware issues with laptops, desktops, printers, and mobile devices.
  Troubleshoot software problems related to operating systems (Windows, macOS), Microsoft 365 and a variety of business applications.
  Assist users with account management, including password resets, access permissions, and onboarding/offboarding.
  Escalate complex issues to Tier 2/3 support or specialized teams while maintaining ownership of the ticket and communication with the user.

 

System Administration & Maintenance (20%)

  Perform the initial setup, configuration, and deployment of new user equipment (laptops, phones, etc.).
  Assist in the management of user accounts and groups in Active Directory or Azure AD.
  Monitor system alerts and perform basic health checks on network connectivity and core services.
  Maintain accurate and up-to-date documentation in the IT knowledge base.

 

Customer Service & Communication (10%)

  Deliver exceptional, empathetic, and patient customer service to users of all technical skill levels.
  Clearly communicate technical information in a user-friendly, understandable manner.
  Manage and prioritize multiple support tickets, ensuring SLAs (Service Level Agreements) are met.
  Follow up with users to ensure their technical issues have been fully resolved.
job_requirements:
Qualifications & Skills

Required:

Bachelor’s degree in information technology, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent work experience.
Proven experience (1-3 years) in a helpdesk or technical support role.
Strong knowledge of Microsoft Windows 10/11 and Microsoft 365 Suite (Outlook, Teams, Word, Excel).
Fundamental understanding of network concepts (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP, VPN).
Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills.
A patient, customer-oriented attitude with a genuine desire to help.

Preferred (Nice-to-Have):

Relevant professional certifications (e.g., CompTIA A+, Network+, Microsoft 365 Fundamentals, ITIL Foundation).
Experience with a professional ticketing system (e.g., Jira Service Management, Fresh service, Zendesk).
Basic experience with Active Directory and Azure AD for user management.
Familiarity with macOS and mobile device management (MDM).
Knowledge of remote desktop support tools.

job_description:
About Codecontent
‍CodeContent is a UK-based technical content company founded by Mostafa Ibrahim, a former software engineer at GoCardless and Arm. We have published 300+ technical articles across 18 clients.
We produce tutorials and deep technical blog posts for leading AI/ML platforms, YC-backed authentication companies, and major cloud infrastructure providers. Our team of 7 is made up of engineers who write, not writers who research. 
We have been operating for 3+ years.

‍About the Role
‍We are hiring a Software Engineer (Technical Content) to build and explain real software and AI systems for developer audiences. This is not a writing-only role. You will implement, run, and validate real code, then turn that work into long-form technical blogs and tutorials for our clients.
You will work across a wide range of topics: system design, LLMs, RAG pipelines, agent-based workflows, APIs, cloud infrastructure, and developer tools. 
The output is written content, but the work is engineering.Your work will be published on the engineering blogs of leading AI/ML platforms, YC-backed startups, and major cloud infrastructure providers, reaching tens of thousands of developers worldwide.

‍Responsibilities 
Write long-form technical blogs and tutorials based on provided briefsImplement, run, and validate real code as part of every article.
Research and clearly explain software engineering and AI workflows to developer audiences.

Cover topics including:
Software engineering fundamentals and system design
AI/ML concepts, LLMs, RAG systems, and agent-based workflows
APIs, cloud infrastructure, and developer tools
Revise drafts based on editorial and technical feedback
Deliver consistently and on time

‍How We Work
‍This is a full-time position (40 hours/week) and your primary professional commitment. We are a small, focused team where every person carries significant ownership.
‍
‍Compensation
EGP 30,000 – EGP 40,000 per month (based on experience)
Direct mentorship from engineers with 300+ published pieces
Fully remote
Exposure to 10+ tech stacks annually
Salary reviews every 6 months based on performance

‍Why This Role
‍This is a role for engineers who enjoy explaining how things work. 
You will spend your days building real systems and writing about them for technical audiences. No fluff, no marketing spin. 
You will gain deep experience writing production-grade technical content.

skills: SAP | Project Management | Finance
job_description:
Position Summary

The SAP Program Manager is accountable for governing and delivering the Group’s SAP S/4HANA program across multiple legal entities. The role ensures reliable day-to-day operations and service continuity, while planning and executing structured rollouts, enhancements, and process standardization initiatives. The SAP Program Manager provides executive-level governance, enforces disciplined scope and change control, and manages vendor performance under the SAP Partner maintenance agreement to achieve measurable outcomes in stability, adoption, compliance, and business value.

Key Responsibilities

Establish and maintain program governance, including steering committee cadence, executive reporting, decision logging, escalation pathways, and integrated program planning across all SAP workstreams.
Own and maintain the SAP roadmap, release strategy, and prioritization model aligned to Group objectives, operational constraints, resource capacity, and risk appetite.
Lead end-to-end delivery of SAP initiatives, including demand intake, requirements governance, solution alignment, dependency management, testing strategy, cutover planning, go-live readiness, and stabilization.
Drive a template-first approach across legal entities where appropriate, ensuring process harmonization while managing justified entity-specific requirements through a controlled exception process.
Implement and enforce robust scope management and formal change control to prevent uncontrolled demand, ensure predictable delivery, and protect operational stability.
Define and enforce the SAP run model and service management practices (incident, problem, change, and release management), ensuring clear ownership, measurable service levels, and continuous improvement in service quality.
Oversee production stability and operational excellence, including recurring issue elimination, root-cause governance, regression prevention, and sustained reduction in critical incidents.
Coordinate closely with infrastructure, security, and SAP Basis stakeholders to ensure appropriate governance over on-premise operations, including availability and performance oversight, patching coordination, backup and disaster recovery readiness, and capacity planning.
Act as the accountable owner for the SAP Partner relationship, including SLA/KPI definition, ticket governance, escalation management, deliverable acceptance, and structured service review cadence with documented actions and outcomes.
Ensure commercial discipline and cost control by correctly classifying work (maintenance vs change), validating estimates and scope, obtaining approvals, and managing change orders to prevent unmanaged scope and hidden costs.
Ensure high-quality documentation and knowledge transfer, including operating procedures, runbooks, support guides, and solution documentation sufficient to reduce critical dependencies on external parties over time.
Serve as the primary interface between executive leadership, entity management, business process owners, internal SAP resources, and the SAP Partner, facilitating timely decisions and aligning stakeholders around clear priorities.
Drive business readiness and adoption through structured communications, key-user engagement, training planning, UAT governance, and readiness sign-offs, ensuring sustained utilization and process compliance.
Support audit and compliance expectations by enforcing access governance principles, segregation of duties controls, traceable change documentation, and appropriate approvals in collaboration with Finance, Internal Audit, and security stakeholders.
Provide planning and coordination for internal SAP resources, ensuring role clarity (RACI), workload balance, cross-training, and standards for “how the Group delivers and runs SAP.”
job_requirements:
Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or equivalent.
Demonstrated experience managing enterprise SAP programs (S/4HANA preferred), including multi-entity governance and delivery.
Proven vendor and contract management experience, including maintenance/AMS agreements, SLAs, service reviews, and escalation management.
Working knowledge of on-premise SAP operational dependencies across infrastructure, security, and Basis functions (hands-on Basis not required).
Strong leadership, stakeholder management, and executive communication skills.

job_description:
Senior Odoo Developer — Integrations & Financial Reporting
About the Project

We are a digital agency (Anecdote) with our in-house development team, running Odoo as the operational hub for project management, campaign workflows, and finance. We maintain custom modules that link external advertising and analytics platforms to Odoo — automating budget tracking, media planning, and performance reporting — and extend Odoo's accounting layer for localised financial statements, asset management, and bank reconciliation. The stack is cloud-hosted in a containerised deployment with CI/CD automation.

 
What You'll Work On

 

Third-party integrations: Maintain and extend connections to advertising and analytics platforms via REST/JSON APIs and their SDKs — handling data sync, TTL-based caching, and robust error handling.
Campaign & project workflows: Build and evolve wizards and automation that translate campaign planning data into structured Odoo work items (tasks, budgets, timelines) and generate QWeb reports with KPI summaries.
Budget & time tracking: Develop models and logic for rolling budget computation, variable service pricing, and time allocation across hierarchical task structures.
Accounting & financial reporting: Extend core Odoo accounting with additional reports (PDF and Excel), financial statements, asset tracking flows, and reconciliation support.
Custom views & OWL components: Create and maintain Kanban/List view extensions, field widgets, and OWL components — including custom styling where needed.
Platform & CI/CD: Work within a Docker-based local dev environment and a CI pipeline that builds, tests, and deploys to a container registry on merge.
Test coverage: Champion automated testing — writing model-level and integration tests to establish a growing safety net across the codebase.

 

Tech Stack

 

ERP: Odoo
Backend: Python, Odoo ORM
Frontend: OWL (Odoo Web Library), JavaScript, SCSS
External APIs: Advertising/marketing platform APIs
Database: PostgreSQL
Reporting: QWeb (PDF), openpyxl (Excel)
Containerisation: Docker, Docker Compose
CI/CD: Bitbucket Pipelines, container registry
 
How We Work

 

Module-first architecture: every feature lives in its own addon with clean __manifest__.py dependency declarations — new work follows the same pattern.
Small, focused team: compact dev team where each engineer owns full vertical slices (model → view → report → deployment).
Docker-native workflow: local dev runs entirely in containers via Docker Compose; there is no bare-metal Odoo setup.
CI on merge: builds are image-cached to keep pipeline times short; deployments are triggered automatically on merge.
Growing test culture: we want someone who will help establish and expand automated test coverage — this is an active priority, not an afterthought.
Code style: consistent use of Odoo decorator patterns and access-control matrices.
job_requirements:
Must-Have Qualifications

 

3+ years of Odoo development.
Python ORM mastery: computed/related fields with @api.depends, @api.[removed], @api.constrains, _sql_constraints, and CRUD overrides (create, write, unlink).
Model inheritance patterns: _inherit (extension), abstract mixins, and TransientModel wizards.
Security model: authoring ir.model.access.csv, record rules (ir.rule), group-based permissions, and knowing when (and when not) to use sudo().
QWeb reports: designing and debugging XML report templates that produce both PDF and structured data output.
External API integration: consuming REST/JSON APIs from Python with proper error handling; hands-on experience with at least one third-party SDK.
Odoo JS ecosystem: custom OWL components, extending standard Kanban/List controllers, understanding the asset bundle pipeline.
Docker: comfortable writing and debugging docker-compose.yml files; able to trace build failures through multi-stage Dockerfile logic.
 
Nice-to-Have

 

Major ad platform APIs (e.g. Meta, Tiktok, Snapchat) — existing familiarity with advertising platform APIs shortens onboarding significantly.
Accounting domain knowledge — understanding of double-entry bookkeeping, asset depreciation schedules, and bank reconciliation flows.
AWS & Bitbucket Pipelines — experience with cloud container registries and pipeline YAML configuration is a plus.
openpyxl / xlsxwriter — used for Excel report exports; ability to produce formatted multi-sheet workbooks.
Bank statement parsing — familiarity with OFX/QIF formats or relevant parsing libraries.
PostgreSQL performance tuning — the reporting layer will benefit from query-level optimisation as data grows.
pytest-odoo or Odoo test runner — experience writing TransactionCase and HttpCase tests; bootstrapping a test suite from scratch is highly valued.

job_description:
Role

 

Dewan Architects & Engineers is a global design consultancy firm with 900+ professionals across offices in the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Vietnam, the Philippines, and Iraq. We are seeking an IT Business Analyst to join our Enterprise Technology function. In this role, you will support business analysis and digital change initiatives across AEC workflows (design, engineering, project delivery, commercial, and support functions), translating business needs into clear requirements and actionable delivery plans. You will partner with stakeholders and delivery teams to implement solutions across core platforms (CDE/BIM, PMIS, ERP, collaboration, and integrations), ensuring adoption, value realization, and governance. You will report to the Head of the Enterprise Technology Department (ETD) and work closely with solution architects, vendors, and BI/Data teams to deliver integrated outcomes.

 

Responsibilities 

 

Lead business analysis activities for assigned initiatives; apply BA standards, templates, and quality controls for requirements and documentation.
Partner with business leaders (Design, PMO, Construction, Commercial, Finance, HR, Procurement) to understand priorities and shape delivery roadmaps.
Report to the Head of ETD and collaborate with solution architects, engineers, vendors, and business owners to deliver fit-for-purpose solutions.
Facilitate discovery workshops to map current-state processes and design future-state workflows across the project lifecycle (tender → design → construction → handover).
Elicit, analyze, and document business, functional, non-functional, and reporting requirements; maintain traceability through delivery and change control.
Translate requirements into epics/user stories, acceptance criteria, and test scenarios for Agile/Hybrid delivery teams and vendors.
Support selection, implementation, and continuous improvement of AEC platforms (e.g., CDE/DMS, BIM coordination, PMIS/field tools, ERP, and collaboration).
Define data needs and KPIs for project and corporate reporting; align with BI/data teams on data sources, definitions, and governance.
Coordinate UAT planning and execution: test scripts, defect triage, go/no-go readiness, and sign-off with key stakeholders.
Drive change management activities: communications, training coordination, adoption tracking, and post-go-live stabilization.
Work with solution architects and engineers on integration requirements (APIs, middleware, data flows) between ERP, PMIS, CDE, and identity/security controls.
Ensure solutions comply with IT policies, security requirements, and project information management standards (including access controls and audit evidence where needed).
Track benefits realization and operational KPIs; identify opportunities for automation and continuous improvement (workflow, RPA, self-service).
Demonstrate ethical and professional behavior and adherence to company policies and procedures.
job_requirements:
Qualifications

 

Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Engineering, Construction Management, or related field (or equivalent experience).
8+ years of experience in business analysis / digital delivery, with demonstrated ownership of BA workstreams in complex programs.
Proven experience delivering solutions in AEC environments, with strong understanding of design and construction workflows.
Strong requirements management skills (BRD/FRD, user stories, process mapping, acceptance criteria, traceability).
Working knowledge of AEC delivery systems such as CDE/document control (ACC/BIM 360, Aconex, SharePoint), PMIS/field tools (Procore, Primavera P6/MS Project), and/or ERP (Oracle/SAP/Dynamics).
Experience with stakeholder management, workshop facilitation, and executive-ready communication.
Hands-on experience supporting UAT and change adoption across multi-location teams.
Willingness to participate in occasional off-hours/weekend support for go-lives and major releases.

 

Bonus Points

 

CBAP/CCBA, PMI-PBA, PMP, PRINCE2, Agile/Scrum, or ITIL certification.
Experience with BIM information management concepts and CDE governance (e.g., ISO 19650-aligned practices).
Familiarity with integration patterns, APIs, and data modeling fundamentals; experience working with middleware/iPaaS tools.

We are building 99, an ethical money account for the UK market. Our mission is simple: give people a transparent, fair, and values aligned way to manage their money. We are a pre seed fintech operating as an EMI agent under UK regulation, which means we take compliance and data protection seriously while moving fast to ship product.
We are establishing an engineering presence in Egypt to build our core platform. This role is the first technical hire in that hub and will set the foundation for everything that follows.
The Role
You will be the Lead Backend Architect for our Egypt Engineering Hub. You will design the systems that power our mobile app, define how we integrate with banking partners, and ensure our architecture can scale from thousands to millions of users. You will work closely with UK leadership on product direction and technical standards.
This is not a hands off architecture role. You will write code, establish engineering practices, and make the final call on technical trade offs. You will also design our shadow ledger, the internal system that tracks every penny moving through our platform and ensures we can reconcile with our banking partners down to the last cent.
What You Will Do
Design and build the core backend services that handle user accounts, transactions, subscriptions, and regulatory reporting
Architect our shadow ledger system, ensuring immutable records, audit trails, and perfect reconciliation with EMI Principal systems
Define database schemas, API contracts, and service boundaries that can evolve as we grow
Establish code review standards and enforce security, performance, and maintainability across the codebase
Collaborate with UK product leadership to translate business requirements into technical specifications
Define infrastructure requirements and deployment patterns that satisfy UK data residency rules
Document architectural decisions, runbooks, and technical specifications in plain English for UK stakeholders

How We Work

Our Egypt hub operates on a zero access model. You will build systems that handle UK customer data, but you will never see that data yourself. We use synthetic data for development, mock servers for integration testing, and automated pipelines for deployment. Production access is restricted by design. You will architect the systems, but production credentials remain with UK based DevOps. This is non negotiable for regulatory compliance, and we need someone who respects those boundaries while still shipping excellent software.

What We Offer

Competitive salary paid monthly (GBP£ Optional)
Performance based bonuses tied to product milestones
Professional development budget for conferences, courses, and certifications
Flexible working hours with core collaboration times
Opportunity to shape the technical foundation of an early stage fintech from the ground up
Direct impact on a product that helps people manage money in line with their values

job_title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer Backend · Frontend · DevOps · Cloud Infrastructure
skills: Cloud | DevOps | Information Technology (IT) | Software Development | Computer Science | Software Engineering
job_description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

DimCarpi GmbH is a Munich-based lifestyle and fashion brand — registered under HRB 304755 — that delivers elegant, functional apparel and accessories to a global audience. As we scale our digital infrastructure, we are building a world-class engineering team in Egypt.

 

We are looking for a Senior Full-Stack Engineer who is genuinely strong across the entire stack: from crafting pixel-perfect, high-performance frontends to designing fault-tolerant backend services and deploying them on modern cloud infrastructure. This is a high-impact, high-ownership role. Your code will be running in production, at scale, from day one.

 

You should care deeply about system reliability, security, and developer experience — not just feature delivery. If you enjoy solving hard infrastructure and architecture problems as much as writing clean application code, this role is built for you.

 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Backend Engineering

Design, build, and maintain production-grade RESTful and GraphQL APIs using Node.js frameworks (Fastify, Express.js, NestJS) or Go (Gin/Echo) or Python (FastAPI)
Architect and implement microservices and event-driven systems that are decoupled, observable, and resilient
Manage database design, migrations, indexing strategies, and query optimization across PostgreSQL and MongoDB
Implement robust authentication and authorization systems — OAuth 2.0, JWT, RBAC, session management
Write well-tested, well-documented backend code with a focus on maintainability and long-term scalability

Frontend Engineering

Build high-performance, accessible frontend applications using React, Next.js (App Router / Server Components), Angular, or Vue.js / Vite
Apply modern state management patterns (Zustand, Redux Toolkit, React Query / TanStack Query)
Ensure excellent Core Web Vitals: optimize for LCP, FID, CLS, and TTI across all pages
Implement responsive, accessible UI using Tailwind CSS or component libraries (MUI, Radix UI)
Collaborate closely with product and design to translate wireframes into production-ready interfaces

Infrastructure & DevOps

Containerize services using Docker and orchestrate workloads with Kubernetes (K8s)
Deploy and operate applications on cloud platforms — AWS (EC2, ECS, RDS, S3, CloudFront, Lambda), Hetzner Cloud, or DigitalOcean / Vultr
Configure and optimize NGINX as a reverse proxy, load balancer, and TLS termination point
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or similar tools
Set up monitoring, alerting, and distributed tracing using Prometheus, Grafana, Sentry, or Datadog
Manage infrastructure as code using Terraform or Pulumi

Performance, Security & Reliability

Identify and eliminate performance bottlenecks across frontend (bundle size, render blocking, lazy loading) and backend (N+1 queries, slow endpoints, inefficient caching)
Implement multi-layer caching strategies: Redis (in-memory), CDN (CloudFront, Cloudflare), and HTTP cache headers
Apply security best practices: input validation and sanitization, protection against OWASP Top 10 (XSS, CSRF, SQL injection, IDOR), secrets management, and dependency auditing
Design for high availability: implement retries, circuit breakers, graceful degradation, health checks, and automated failover
Define and monitor SLOs and uptime targets; own incident response for services you build

 

TECH STACK & TOOLS

Backend Frameworks

Node.js: Fastify · Express.js · NestJS    |    Python: FastAPI    |   Go: Gin · Echo    |    .NET (C#): ASP.NET Core

Frontend Frameworks

React · Next.js (App Router) · Angular · Vue.js / Vite · TypeScript

Databases

PostgreSQL · MongoDB · Redis · Prisma ORM · TypeORM · Mongoose

Cloud & Infrastructure

AWS (EC2, ECS, RDS, S3, CloudFront, Lambda, SQS) · Hetzner Cloud · DigitalOcean · Cloudflare

DevOps & Tooling

Docker · Kubernetes · NGINX · Terraform · GitHub Actions · GitLab CI · Prometheus · Grafana · Sentry · Datadog · ELK Stack

Other

GraphQL · REST · WebSockets · RabbitMQ / Kafka · Git · Linux (Ubuntu/Debian)

 

 

 

ABOUT DIMCARPI GMBH

DimCarpi GmbH is a Munich-based lifestyle and fashion brand committed to combining elegant design with practical functionality. We operate internationally, with a growing engineering team in Egypt driving the digital side of the business.

 

We believe great software is built by engineers who have context, ownership, and space to do their best work. We are building that kind of team.

 

Registered under HRB 304755, Munich, Germany.

 

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. We evaluate candidates on demonstrated ability and practical experience, not credentials alone.
job_requirements:
REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

2 – 5 years of hands-on professional experience in full-stack software development
Deep working knowledge of at least one backend framework (Fastify, NestJS, FastAPI, Gin, or ASP.NET Core)
Solid frontend experience with React or Next.js — you understand how the browser renders and optimizes content, not just how to write components
Strong SQL fundamentals: schema design, normalization, indexing, query planning, and performance tuning in PostgreSQL
Experience with MongoDB: schema design for document-oriented data, aggregation pipelines, indexing strategies
Practical experience with Docker — containerizing applications, writing multi-stage Dockerfiles, running Docker Compose environments
Familiarity with at least one cloud platform — AWS, Hetzner, or equivalent — for deploying and operating live production services
Understanding of web performance: what affects load times, how to measure it (Lighthouse, WebPageTest, Core Web Vitals), and how to fix it
Solid grasp of web security principles: OWASP Top 10, secure coding practices, secrets management, and dependency vulnerability awareness
Experience writing unit, integration, and end-to-end tests (Jest, Vitest, Playwright, Cypress, or equivalent)
Strong command of English for asynchronous and synchronous communication with an international team

 

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

Experience with Kubernetes: deploying services, managing pods and namespaces, writing Helm charts
Hands-on experience with NGINX: configuring reverse proxies, rate limiting, SSL/TLS termination, and load balancing
Experience with Hetzner Cloud or similar European cloud providers in addition to or instead of AWS
Exposure to Go (.NET) or Python in a backend production context
Familiarity with message brokers (RabbitMQ, Kafka, or AWS SQS/SNS) for asynchronous processing
Experience in e-commerce, SaaS, or high-traffic consumer applications
Knowledge of observability practices: structured logging, distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry), and metrics dashboards
Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field (or equivalent practical experience)

 

WHO YOU ARE

You take ownership — when you ship something, you care about what happens after it goes live
You communicate clearly and proactively, especially in a remote-first environment
You think in systems, not just features — you consider how your code affects reliability, observability, and other engineers
You are curious and self-directed: you do not wait to be told what to learn
You are comfortable with ambiguity and can scope and prioritize your own work
You give and receive feedback professionally and constructively
You are persistent — you debug hard problems methodically and see them through to resolution

 

WHAT WE OFFER

Competitive salary benchmarked against international engineering markets
Equity / profit-sharing opportunities as the team scales
Fully remote role with flexible working hours aligned to a Central European timezone
Direct access to senior leadership and meaningful technical influence over product and architecture decisions
A small, focused team where your contributions are visible and impactful
Work on a real, live product used by customers — not internal tooling
Opportunities to grow into technical lead or architect roles
Budget for learning resources, courses, and conferences

job_description:
AI-Native Full-Stack Developer (Corporate Innovation Role)
Headline: [REMOTE] AI-Native Developer – Corporate Innovation Role –
Target Profile: Talented young developers in Egypt with an elite corporate mindset and exceptional English.
Timezone Requirement: Minimum 10 hours of "Desk Time" per week, overlapping with AST (Atlantic Standard Time) for strategic coordination with Trinidad & Tobago-based leadership.

The Opportunity

We are seeking a high-caliber, young developer to join our team as a core technical engine. This is a long-term professional role focused on building limited-deployment agentic business systems for very niche micro-markets.
You will work under the supervision of our Technical Lead to develop, deploy, and maintain a suite of agentic deployments, full-stack Micro-SaaS products, and internal projects.
The Profile: "AI-Native Professional"
We are looking for a developer who is aligned with operating as part of a high-performance, English-speaking corporate team. You should be someone who doesn't just "write code," but understands how to use AI to solve complex business problems at 10x speed.

Responsibilities

Deployment & Maintenance: Orchestrate and manage autonomous AI agents for internal operations and external client-facing stacks.
High-Leverage Execution: Build out architectures supporting operations in Insurance, Law, Fiduciary work, and Auditing.
Collaboration: Participate in 10 hours of weekly "Desk Time" for sprint planning, live debugging, and coordination.

The Deal

Starting Salary: $500 - 650 USD per month (Contractual).
Performance Culture: We offer a high-trust environment. Delivery of agreed outcomes is the key consideration.
Payment Security: Monthly salary is lodged in advance via Escrow and released upon achievement of monthly milestones and verified performance.

How to Apply

We value transparency and demonstration of skill over traditional CVs.

Your application MUST include:

Portfolio Pieces: Links to your GitHub repositories or live projects.
Project Descriptions (Video Preferred): A Loom video (or similar screen recording) walking us through a specific project you built.
Explain the problem, the tech stack, and specifically how you utilized AI Agents to accelerate the development process.
Statement of Intent: A brief explanation of why you are interested in building niche agentic systems for the Caribbean market.

Deadline for application is Wednesday 25th March 2026
job_requirements:
Exceptional English: Outstanding spoken and written English is mandatory. You must be able to document work clearly and communicate complex technical concepts in a corporate setting.
Corporate Mindset: You are disciplined, self-directed, and understand the importance of reliability, reporting, and consistent "Desk Time" presence.
AI-First Workflow: Expert-level orchestration of AI agents (Cursor, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, etc.). We value the result over the manual hours spent.
Technical Breadth: Proficiency in modern stacks (React/Next.js, Python, Supabase) and a deep interest in Agentic AI and ERP implementations.

skills: Data Engineering | Python | Scala | Databricks | Snowflake | SQL | Relational Databases | Cloud Platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP)
job_description:
Role Summary

Take the next step in your career by joining a team where you will not just build pipelines, but design scalable data platforms. We are looking for an experienced Data Engineer who combines strong technical expertise in Python and SQL with a mindset for optimization. You will work directly with clients to build modern Data Warehouses and Lakehouses using industry-standard tools like Databricks and Azure Data Factory.

 

The Mission

To build and optimize the data backbone of our clients' businesses. Your mission goes beyond moving data; you will ensure that pipelines are resilient, scalable, and economically efficient (FinOps). You will bridge the gap between infrastructure and data, taking ownership of technical delivery and helping shape the architecture of modern data platforms.

 

The Tech Stack

Core Languages: Python or Scala (Proficient/OOP), SQL (Advanced/Analytical).
Compute & Storage: Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery, Synapse, Redshift.
Orchestration: Azure Data Factory (ADF), Airflow, Dagster.
Transformation: dbt (Core/Cloud), Spark/PySpark (Optimization focus).
Infrastructure: Terraform (Implementation), Docker, Kubernetes (K8s).

 

Your Responsibilities

Data Platform Development: Design and implement pipelines to ingest, clean, and transform data into Data Lakes and Data Warehouses.
Performance & FinOps: Actively monitor and optimize pipelines for performance and cost. Differentiate between a working query and an efficient one.
Deployment & Configuration: Configure pipeline deployments and manage environment settings using IaC tools (Terraform) and CI/CD.
Quality Assurance: Implement data quality checks (e.g., Great Expectations, dbt tests) and ensure data integrity.
Mentorship: Communicate with stakeholders to clarify requirements and provide technical guidance/code reviews to Junior engineers.

 

What We Offer:

Long-term career stability with a competitive salary paid in USD.
Conditions for steady career development.
Development supported by dedicated mentors and a variety of programs focused on expertise and innovation.
Private medical insurance provided after successful completion of the probationary period
A well-equipped and cozy office supports comfort and productivity across all project stages.
Welcoming atmosphere and a friendly corporate culture.
job_requirements:
Your Skills

Experience: 2+ years of hands-on data engineering experience (ETL/ELT).
Data Architecture: Strong understanding of Data Warehousing concepts (Star/Snowflake Schema) and Data Lake principles (Medallion Architecture).
Key Platforms: Proven experience with Databricks (managing clusters, notebooks), Azure Data Factory (pipelines, data flows), Snowflake.
Engineering Core: Proficiency in Python (OOP, functional programming) and strong SQL skills for complex transformations.
Cloud Fluency: Practical experience with at least one major cloud (AWS, GCP, Azure) and its data services (e.g., Kinesis/Lambda, Dataflow/BigQuery, Synapse).
Infrastructure Awareness: Base understanding of Infrastructure as Code (Terraform). Ability to deploy and modify infrastructure resources independently.
Communication: Ability to communicate technical decisions directly to clients and the team.

 

Nice to Have

Advanced IaC: Experience setting up complex modules in Terraform or Pulumi.
Lakehouse Formats: Hands-on experience with Delta Lake or Apache Iceberg features (Time Travel, Schema Evolution).
NoSQL: Experience modeling data for NoSQL databases (MongoDB, DynamoDB).
Certifications: Associate-level cloud certifications (e.g., Azure Data Engineer Associate, Databricks Certified Data Engineer).

job_description:
About the Role

We are seeking an engineer who doesn't just "talk" to LLMs but builds autonomous, resilient systems. You will design multi-agent architecture that can reason, use tools, and recover from failures independently.
 

Your primary goal is to bridge the gap between "cool demos" and "production-grade reliability." You will be responsible for deploying agents across various business sectors (e.g., Finance, Operations, Customer Success), ensuring they are not only intelligent but also safe, cost-effective, and predictable in a production environment.
 

Key Responsibilities
 

●  Agent Orchestration: Design and implement stateful, multi-turn agent workflows using frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, PydanticAI, Swarm, Haystack, or Bee Agent Framework.
 

● Proactive Reliability & Guardrails: Architect systems that prevent production meltdowns. Implement circuit breakers, "human-in-the-loop" triggers, and input/output guardrails to stop infinite loops, prompt injections, and hallucinated tool calls before they reach the end user.
 

● Multi-Sector Tooling: Build and maintain high-precision API integrations (tools) that allow agents to interact with diverse business systems (ERPs, CRMs, custom databases) deterministically.
 

● Observability & Tracing: Set up advanced tracing (e.g., LangSmith, Langfuse, or Arize Phoenix) to debug complex reasoning chains and monitor agent trajectories in real-time.
 

● Rigorous Evaluation (Evals): Develop automated "Golden Datasets" and evaluation frameworks (using RAGas, DeepEval, G-Eval, LangSmith Evaluators, or custom model-based grading) to measure agent success rates and prevent regressions before every deployment.
 

● Cost & Latency Optimization: Manage the "token budget" by implementing tiered model routing (e.g., using Gemini 3 Flash for initial reasoning and Ultra for final verification) and optimizing context window usage.
job_requirements:
Required Qualifications
 

●  3+ years of Software Engineering experience, with a background in Python and FastAPI (or similar).
 

● 1+ year of experience specifically focused on LLM-based Agents in a production environment.
 

● Deep Expertise in Agentic Patterns: Proven experience implementing ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, or Multi-Agent Supervisor architectures.
 

● Production Infrastructure: Hands-on experience with modern Vector Databases and Search Engines (e.g., Pinecone, Milvus, Weaviate, Qdrant, Chroma, or pgvector) and semantic caching strategies.

job_description:
As a Principal AI Architect, you will define and govern the end-to-end technical architecture of Makkook’s Optimization & Decision Intelligence platform(s), and ensure we can deliver new customer implementations fast (target: ≤ 8 weeks per use case after core platform maturity).
Core Responsibilities:
Platform Architecture & Technical Strategy
Define platform architecture: modular components, data contracts, integration patterns, deployment topology (cloud/on-prem), and security/observability standards.
Design reusable “platform primitives”: canonical entities (resources/tasks/time/cost), constraint packs, objective templates, scenario manager, and explainability layer.
Make solver/stack decisions (build vs buy), and establish technical roadmaps with the CTO.
Optimization & Decision Engine Leadership
Lead modeling and solution strategy for key families:
Production planning & scheduling (sequence-dependent setups/changeovers, minimum run lengths, maintenance windows)
Workforce allocation and shift planning
Routing, dispatching, and fleet assignment
Inventory/replenishment and supply chain planning
Set standards for model formulation, performance, scalability, and stability (runtime budgets, warm starts, decomposition, heuristics/hybrids where needed).
Build a repeatable benchmarking harness and KPI framework (cost, service, risk, feasibility rate, runtime).
Feasibility, Diagnostics & Explainability
Own the approach to infeasibility detection and repair (constraint relaxation strategies, IIS where applicable, actionable diagnostics).
Ensure decisions are explainable to operations teams (constraints hit, trade-offs, what changed vs previous plan, reason codes).
Delivery & Engineering Excellence
Partner with PM/Delivery to define scope, milestones, acceptance criteria, and technical risk management.
Guide integrations with ERP/WMS/TMS/CMMS/SCADA and production data pipelines.
Establish OptOps practices: versioning, monitoring, runtime regressions, rollback, and quality gates.
Technical Leadership
Mentor AI engineers (including those coming from NLP/CV backgrounds) into OR/Optimization craftsmanship.
Run architecture reviews, ADRs, and enforce best practices across code, models, and deployments.
job_requirements:
7+ years in applied optimization / operations research / decision intelligence, with hands-on delivery experience.
Strong track record in building and deploying optimization systems using one or more of:
MILP / MIP, CP/CP-SAT, decomposition, heuristics/metaheuristics, hybrid strategies.
Ability to translate real operations into formal models (capacities, time windows, setup/changeover, batching, minimum run lengths, resource calendars, SLAs).
Solid software engineering fundamentals for production systems (clean architecture, testing, CI/CD mindset, performance profiling).
Experience integrating decision engines into real enterprise environments (APIs, event-driven patterns, data pipelines, on-prem constraints).
Clear communication: can explain complex trade-offs to both engineers and operators.
Hands-on experience in: OR-Tools (CP-SAT), Gurobi/CPLEX, Pyomo, JuMP, or similar.
Experience with feasibility diagnostics (IIS, relaxation, reason codes, repair recommendations).
Experience in building “re-optimization loops” (daily/shift updates, warm starts, rolling-horizon planning).
Observability/monitoring: Prometheus/Grafana (or equivalent), structured logging, runtime regression monitoring.
Preferred to have domain exposure in manufacturing planning, logistics routing, retail replenishment, or supply chain optimization.

job_description:
Employment Type: Full-time (or Senior Contractor)
Reporting to: Head of Managed Services / Technical Lead
 

Role Summary
We are seeking an experienced Senior Database Administrator to lead database vendor-support activities and provide expert-level operational, performance, and architectural support across a heterogeneous environment (MongoDB, MySQL/MariaDB, Microsoft SQL Server, and others). This hands-on role is responsible for resolving critical tickets, driving stability and performance improvements, coordinating vendor escalations, and mentoring junior DBAs. The Senior DBA will be a key escalation point for complex incidents and lead database-related projects including migrations, HA designs, and automation.
 

Key Responsibilities

Serve as primary escalation engineer for complex database incidents and SLA breaches; lead incident response and post-incident root-cause analysis (RCA).
Troubleshoot and resolve high-severity tickets across MongoDB, MySQL/MariaDB, MS SQL Server, and other DBMS; coordinate cross-team and vendor escalations as required.
Design, implement and maintain high-availability, replication, clustering, sharding, and disaster recovery solutions; lead DR planning and periodic DR tests.
Lead performance tuning efforts: query optimization, indexing strategy, resource contention mitigation, and capacity planning for large-scale production systems.
Architect and execute database migrations and upgrades (on-prem ↔ cloud, cloud-to-cloud, major version upgrades), ensuring minimal downtime and reliable rollback plans.
Define and enforce database change control processes; review and approve schema changes and major configuration adjustments.
Implement and maintain robust backup/restore strategies (including point-in-time recovery) and ensure recoverability through regular restore testing.
Develop and maintain database security best practices: roles and permissions, encryption at rest/in transit, auditing, and compliance controls.
Build and improve observability and monitoring (alerts, dashboards, anomaly detection) using tools such as Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, New Relic, Percona Monitoring, or vendor solutions.
Automate repetitive operational tasks and integrate database operations into CI/CD pipelines using scripting (Python, Bash) and IaC tools (Ansible, Terraform).
Collaborate with application, infrastructure, and security teams to design scalable data architectures that meet performance, reliability, and compliance requirements.
Maintain thorough documentation: runbooks, SOPs, capacity forecasts, architecture diagrams, and vendor escalation procedures.
Mentor and coach junior DBAs and support engineers; lead knowledge transfer and run regular training sessions.
Participate in on-call rotation for critical incidents and provide after-hours support when required.
job_requirements:
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, or equivalent experience.
4+ years of hands-on DBA experience with strong, demonstrable expertise in at least two of: MongoDB, MySQL/MariaDB, Microsoft SQL Server.
Proven track record of resolving complex production incidents and driving long-term improvements.
Deep SQL expertise and experience optimizing complex queries, indexing strategies, and execution plans; familiarity with MongoDB query optimization and sharding strategies.
Strong experience with HA, replication, clustering, sharding, and disaster recovery design and operations.
Hands-on experience with cloud-managed database services (AWS RDS/Aurora, Azure SQL, Google Cloud SQL, MongoDB Atlas) and hybrid cloud deployments.
Experience with database migration projects, schema evolution strategies, and zero-downtime deployment techniques.
Proficiency with monitoring/observability tools and log analysis; strong diagnostic skills for performance and resource-related issues.
Scripting/automation skills (Python, Bash, PowerShell) and experience with configuration management / IaC (Ansible, Terraform, CI/CD tooling).
Solid understanding of Linux and Windows server environments, storage, and networking as they relate to database performance.
Excellent communication skills; comfortable coordinating with vendors (MongoDB, Microsoft, Oracle, etc.) and presenting technical information to stakeholders.
Strong documentation skills and commitment to operational excellence and compliance.
English proficiency required for vendor interaction and technical documentation.

Preferred / Nice-to-have

Certifications: MongoDB DBA, Microsoft Certified: Azure Database Administrator, MySQL/MariaDB certifications, or similar.
Experience with containerized databases and Kubernetes operators (e.g., MongoDB Kubernetes Operator, MySQL Operator).
Familiarity with observability platforms (ELK/EFK stacks), query profilers, and advanced performance tools (Query Store, EXPLAIN ANALYZE).
Prior experience working with global enterprise customers, RFP-managed services, or partner ecosystems (e.g., Dell Technologies).
Experience with security and compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, SOC2, PCI-DSS).

Deliverables & KPIs

SLA adherence and mean time to resolution (MTTR) for high-severity incidents.
Reduction in recurring incidents (trend-based KPIs) after RCA implementations.
Successful execution of migrations/upgrades with minimal downtime.
Availability and reliability metrics for database platforms (uptime, replication lag).
Timeliness and quality of runbooks, documentation, and knowledge transfer sessions.

The Root-CA Security Systems Administrator is responsible for the overall health, monitoring, and maintenance of the physical security systems supporting the Root Certificate Authority (Root-CA). Manage and operate physical security systems, including access control, video surveillance (CCTV), intrusion detection, and environmental monitoring systems.
Administer and maintain data center physical infrastructure such as UPS, HVAC (temperature control), fire suppression, and water leak detection systems.
Continuously monitor system health, manage alarms, perform backups, and archive logs/events.
Diagnose and troubleshoot system access issues, hardware/software faults, and component failures.

Support the implementation of security-related projects, including system upgrades, installations, and integrations.

Review and audit system logs regularly to ensure compliance with security policies and standards.

Maintain up-to-date technical documentation, including system configurations, backup records, and maintenance logs.
Report security incidents, system outages, and risks in a timely and structured manner.
Coordinate with internal teams, management, and external vendors to resolve issues and enhance system performance.
Continuously assess and improve physical security controls and operational procedures.

job_description:
About Us & The Role: We’re an ambitious IT System Integrator startup on a mission to secure and connect businesses across Egypt from growing SMBs and hospitals to fast-paced FinTechs. We aren't just looking to fill a seat; we're looking for a core pillar of our business.

We need a Senior IT Sales Manager who thrives on the thrill of the hunt. If you know your way around Network Infrastructure and Cybersecurity, and want the autonomy to build and lead a winning sales team from the ground up, we should definitely talk.

 

What You’ll Actually Be Doing:

Be Our Frontline Hunter: You won't just be managing existing accounts. You'll be out there identifying opportunities, opening new channels, and bringing in fresh B2B clients.
Build Your Squad: Step into a true leadership role. You’ll manage, mentor, and inspire our internal sales team, setting the pace and guiding them to hit their targets.
Sell Solutions, Not Just Boxes: Sit down with clients, dive into their technical headaches, and pitch the right Network Infrastructure (like firewalls, routing, and access points) and Cybersecurity setups that actually solve their problems.
Own the Cycle: Manage everything from that first proactive outreach to closing the deal and maintaining a great relationship afterward.
Shape Our Future: You won’t be taking orders from afar. You’ll sit directly with the founders to brainstorm our market strategy and decide exactly how we position ourselves in the market.

 

Who You Are:

You’ve Done This Before: You have a proven track record of closing B2B deals in Egypt's System Integration (SI) space, ideally at a senior or managerial level.
You Speak the Language: You confidently understand Network Infrastructure and Cybersecurity solutions, and you know how to translate tech features into business value.
You’re Relentlessly Driven: You have strong networking and negotiation skills, and a persistent drive to win new business. To you, a "no" is just a puzzle to solve.
You’re a Natural Leader: You have the experience (or a very strong readiness) to build, manage, and motivate a high-performing sales team.

 

Why You Should Join Us:

Your Earning Potential is in Your Hands: We offer a solid base, but the real reward is in our flexible, uncapped commission structure. You hunt, you close, you earn. Simple.
A Seat at the Table: You aren't joining a rigid corporate machine. You're coming in early to help build the foundation and grow into executive leadership.
True Flexibility: We care about the revenue you bring in and the team you build, not micro-managing your day. Enjoy a hybrid work model built entirely on trust and results.
job_requirements:
Are You the Ideal Candidate?

We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced sales leader who possesses the following qualifications:

Experience: 5-10 years of experience in IT sales, with a proven track record of success.
Leadership: Demonstrated ability to lead, mentor, and motivate a sales team.
Skills: Deep understanding of System Integration, Network Security, Cybersecurity, Sales, B2B Sales, and Networking.
Technical Acumen: [Preferred] Strong technical knowledge of IT products and services.
Communication: Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills.
Strategic Thinking: Ability to develop and execute strategic sales plans.
Education: Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, BIS, Engineering or a related field is preferred.
Additional Skills: Proficiency in strategic planning, client relationship management, sales forecasting, solution selling, negotiation, and risk management.
